rationale: The cited paper is used for a specific methodological function, not just listed as background. In Study 1, the authors explicitly adopt a coding scheme from Ahluwalia (2008) to classify participants' open-ended thoughts as relational versus nonrelational, and this variable is then used in mediation tests central to their mechanism claims. That indicates meaningful use of the cited work in measurement and process testing.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Ahluwalia (2008)'s relational-thought coding approach to operationalize whether participants link the parent brand to the extension. It uses this borrowed measure to test the process mechanism behind mating mind-set effects on extension fit and evaluation.
